title: Create a new project and land in the Editor
description: Verifies a user can create a project from the dashboard and is redirected to the editor view.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill Project Alpha into the project name field
Submit the create project action
Verify the editor layout is displayed

title: Project name is required to create a project
description: Verifies creating a project without a name shows a validation prompt and does not enter the editor.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill  into the project name field
Submit the create project action
Verify a required-name validation error is visible

title: Editor primary layout loads after project creation
description: Verifies the editor shows the expected primary regions after creating a project.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill Editor Layout Project into the project name field
Submit the create project action
Verify the asset sidebar, canvas area, and scene list are displayed

title: Canvas supports selecting and transforming an inserted image element
description: Verifies that an image dropped onto the canvas can be selected and moved/resized via canvas handles.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill Canvas Transform Project into the project name field
Submit the create project action
Drag and drop a valid image file from the OS onto the canvas
Select the newly added canvas element
Move the selected element on the canvas
Resize the selected element using the canvas resize handles
Verify the element remains visible on the canvas after moving and resizing

title: Unsupported file types dropped onto the canvas are rejected
description: Verifies that dropping an unsupported file type does not add an element to the canvas.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill Unsupported Drop Project into the project name field
Submit the create project action
Drag and drop an unsupported file type from the OS onto the canvas
Verify no new canvas element is added

title: Load a local asset folder and view filtered assets in the sidebar
description: Verifies selecting a folder via the asset sidebar loads a filtered list of supported assets.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill Asset Folder Project into the project name field
Submit the create project action
Click the add-folder action in the asset sidebar
Select a local folder containing supported media files in the folder picker
Verify a list of supported assets is displayed in the asset sidebar

title: Drag an asset from the sidebar onto the canvas
description: Verifies an asset loaded in the sidebar can be dragged onto the canvas and appears as a new element.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill Drag Sidebar Asset Project into the project name field
Submit the create project action
Click the add-folder action in the asset sidebar
Select a local folder containing supported media files in the folder picker
Drag an asset from the asset sidebar onto the canvas
Verify a new element appears on the canvas

title: Canceling folder selection does not add assets
description: Verifies canceling the folder picker results in no assets being added to the sidebar.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Fill Cancel Folder Project into the project name field
Submit the create project action
Click the add-folder action in the asset sidebar
Cancel the folder selection dialog
Verify no assets are displayed in the asset sidebar

title: Create a new scene, activate it, and reorder scenes
description: Verifies users can add a scene, switch the active scene, and reorder scenes in the timeline with the order updating.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add a new scene in the scene list
Click a different scene in the scene list to make it active
Drag a scene block in the scene list to a new position
Verify the canvas updates when the selected scene changes
Verify the scene order in the scene list reflects the reorder

title: Add a black-screen active pause and confirm duration cannot be edited
description: Verifies users can insert a black-screen pause and that pause duration is fixed after creation.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add an active pause in the scene list
Select the black-screen pause option from the pause menu
Click the newly added pause block in the scene list
Attempt to edit the pause duration
Verify the pause is previewed on the canvas
Verify the pause duration cannot be modified

title: Toggle transition type between two scenes
description: Verifies the transition badge between scenes can be toggled and the displayed transition type changes.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add a new scene in the scene list
Click the transition badge between two adjacent scenes
Verify the transition type indicator between scenes changes

title: Add a pause and reorder it within the scene list
description: Verifies a pause block can be moved within the timeline sequence and the displayed order updates.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add an active pause in the scene list
Select the solid-color pause option from the pause menu
Drag the pause block to a different position in the scene list
Verify the sequence of scene and pause blocks reflects the reorder

title: Switching between a scene and a pause updates the canvas preview
description: Verifies selecting different block types (scene vs pause) updates the canvas preview accordingly.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add an active pause in the scene list
Select the black-screen pause option from the pause menu
Click a scene block in the scene list
Click the pause block in the scene list
Verify the canvas preview changes when switching between the scene and the pause

title: Add a VHS pause and preview the VHS animation on the canvas
description: Verifies users can create a VHS pause and preview the VHS noise animation in the canvas.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add an active pause in the scene list
Select the VHS pause option from the pause menu
Click the newly added pause block in the scene list
Verify a VHS noise preview fills the canvas area

title: Add a solid-color pause and confirm duration cannot be modified
description: Verifies users can create a solid-color pause and that its duration is not editable after creation.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add an active pause in the scene list
Select the solid-color pause option from the pause menu
Click the newly added pause block in the scene list
Attempt to change the pause duration after creation
Verify a solid-color preview fills the canvas area
Verify the pause duration cannot be modified

title: Add and preview a black-screen pause fills the canvas
description: Verifies black-screen pause preview renders as a full-canvas pause state.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add an active pause in the scene list
Select the black-screen pause option from the pause menu
Click the pause block in the scene list
Verify a black-screen preview fills the canvas area

title: Switch between multiple pause types and confirm preview changes accordingly
description: Verifies selecting different pause blocks results in different canvas previews.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the control to add an active pause in the scene list
Select the black-screen pause option from the pause menu
Click the control to add an active pause in the scene list
Select the VHS pause option from the pause menu
Click the black-screen pause block in the scene list
Click the VHS pause block in the scene list
Verify the canvas preview changes when switching between different pause types

title: Element timeline appears for a scene with elements and updates visibility when handles are adjusted
description: Verifies the element timeline shows tracks for elements and adjusting start/end handles changes the element's visibility window.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Add a text element to the active scene's canvas
Drag the left handle of the element track to adjust the start time
Drag the right handle of the element track to adjust the end time
Verify the element timeline panel is displayed with a track for the element
Verify the element visibility updates according to the adjusted visibility window

title: Element timeline is not shown (or shows empty state) for a scene with no elements
description: Verifies that scenes without elements do not show element tracks and the timeline area is absent or empty.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Ensure the active scene has no elements by clearing all elements from the canvas
Verify the element timeline panel is not displayed or shows an empty state

title: Element timeline shows multiple tracks when multiple elements exist
description: Verifies multiple elements in a scene are represented as separate tracks in the element timeline.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Add a text element to the active scene's canvas
Add an image element to the active scene's canvas
Verify the element timeline panel is displayed
Verify multiple element tracks are displayed

title: Adjusting an element start handle does not remove the element track
description: Verifies start-time adjustment preserves the element track and keeps the timeline usable.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Add a text element to the active scene's canvas
Drag the left handle of the element track to a new position
Verify the element track remains visible in the timeline
Verify the element remains present in the scene and responds to the updated visibility window

title: Adjusting an element end handle does not remove the element track
description: Verifies end-time adjustment preserves the element track and keeps the timeline usable.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Add a text element to the active scene's canvas
Drag the right handle of the element track to a new position
Verify the element track remains visible in the timeline
Verify the element remains present in the scene and responds to the updated visibility window

title: Add and style text, apply animation, and split into individual characters
description: Verifies a user can add a text element, change typography settings, select an animation effect, and split the text into per-character elements on the canvas.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the header control to open the lettering panel
Click the control to add a text element to the canvas
Fill in the text field with LetteringSplitTest
Select a different font family from the font dropdown
Adjust the font size slider to change the size noticeably
Pick a new text color using the color picker
Select an animation effect option
Click the control to split the text into individual letters
Verify the canvas shows the text split into individual character elements
Verify the lettering panel remains open with typography controls available

title: Prevent adding text when no scene is active
description: Verifies the app prompts the user to select or create an active scene before adding a text element when none is active.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the header control to open the lettering panel
Click the control to add a text element to the canvas
Verify a prompt is displayed indicating an active scene must be selected or created before adding text
Verify no new text element is added to the canvas

title: Open the lettering panel from the editor header
description: Verifies the lettering panel can be opened and its primary controls are visible.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the header control to open the lettering panel
Verify the lettering panel is displayed
Verify controls for adding a text element and changing typography settings are available

title: Change font family affects the selected text element
description: Verifies changing the font family updates the appearance of the selected text on the canvas.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the header control to open the lettering panel
Click the control to add a text element to the canvas
Fill in the text field with FontFamilyChange
Select a different font family from the font dropdown
Verify the text element on the canvas reflects the newly selected font family

title: Adjust font size via slider updates text size on canvas
description: Verifies the font size slider changes the size of the selected text element.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the header control to open the lettering panel
Click the control to add a text element to the canvas
Fill in the text field with SizeSliderTest
Adjust the font size slider to a much larger value
Verify the text element appears larger on the canvas

title: Pick text color updates the text element color on canvas
description: Verifies selecting a new color in the color picker updates the selected text element color.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the header control to open the lettering panel
Click the control to add a text element to the canvas
Fill in the text field with ColorPickerTest
Pick a different text color using the color picker
Verify the text element on the canvas reflects the newly selected color

title: Select different animation effects updates the selected effect state
description: Verifies switching between animation effect options updates the current effect selection for the text element.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the header control to open the lettering panel
Click the control to add a text element to the canvas
Fill in the text field with AnimationEffectTest
Select one animation effect option
Select a different animation effect option
Verify the currently selected animation effect reflects the last option chosen

title: Split into letters is blocked or no-ops for empty text
description: Verifies attempting to split when the text field is empty does not create per-character elements and shows a validation prompt or keeps the canvas unchanged.

steps.description: Navigate to /
Click the header control to open the lettering panel
Click the control to add a text element to the canvas
Fill in the text field with
Click the control to split the text into individual letters
Verify the canvas does not show per-character split elements for an empty text input
Verify a validation prompt is shown or the split action is prevented
